questions.subject: Environmental Engineering
questions.stem: A town of population $1,00,000$ has a per-capita demand of $135\,\text{lpcd}$. The average daily demand is _____ MLD.

questions.subject: Environmental Engineering
questions.stem: A town of population $50,000$ has a per-capita demand of $150\,\text{lpcd}$. The average daily demand is _____ MLD.

questions.subject: Environmental Engineering
questions.stem: The ultimate BOD of a waste is $300\,\text{mg/L}$ with deoxygenation rate $k=0.1\,\text{day}^{-1}$ (base 10). The BOD exerted in $5$ days is _____ mg/L.

questions.subject: Environmental Engineering
questions.stem: By the arithmetic-increase method, a town of $50,000$ growing at $1000$ persons/decade over $3$ decades reaches a population of _____.

questions.subject: Environmental Engineering
questions.stem: In an ideal (Type I) sedimentation tank, a particle is removed if its settling velocity is
options: greater than or equal to the surface overflow rate
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Removal when $v_s \ge$ surface loading (overflow) rate.

**Working.**
$v_s \ge SOR$.

**Answer.** ≥ overflow rate

questions.subject: Environmental Engineering
questions.stem: If $5\,\text{mg/L}$ of chlorine is applied and the residual is $0.5\,\text{mg/L}$, the chlorine demand is _____ mg/L.

questions.subject: Environmental Engineering
questions.stem: If $4\,\text{mg/L}$ of chlorine is applied and the residual is $0.3\,\text{mg/L}$, the chlorine demand is _____ mg/L.

questions.subject: Environmental Engineering
questions.stem: Hardness of water is generally expressed in terms of equivalent
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Hardness reported as mg/L of CaCO₃ equivalent.

**Working.**
CaCO₃.

**Answer.** CaCO₃ equivalent

questions.subject: Environmental Engineering
questions.stem: Two identical machines each produce $80\,\text{dB}$ at a point. The combined sound level is _____ dB.

questions.subject: Environmental Engineering
questions.stem: Which of the following are primary air pollutants (emitted directly)?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** SO₂, CO, NO are primary; ground-level ozone is a secondary pollutant.

**Working.**
Options 1, 2, 4.

**Answer.** SO₂, CO, NO
